name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax Title Loans Dillon, South Carolina — TitleMax is a title loan lender offering secured and unsecured lines of credit with same-day funding, bili...
description_long:
The Memphis Summer Avenue TitleMax location has been serving the greater Memphis area since February 2007, situated on Summer Avenue across from AutoZone. TitleMax operates physical storefronts across multiple US states and is among the most widely recognized names in the title loan industry. This location offers bilingual service and is open six days a week, Monday through Saturday.

TitleMax provides three distinct financial products at this location. The title pledge is a secured loan using the borrower's clear vehicle title as collateral; the flex line of credit is a secured revolving credit facility also backed by vehicle title, allowing withdrawals all at once or incrementally up to a credit limit. A third product—an unsecured flexible line of credit—is available both in-store and online, requiring a valid ID, a recent bank statement, and proof of income if recurring deposits are not visible. Online applications for the unsecured product are restricted to eleven states: AZ, DE, ID, KS, MO, MS, SC, TN, TX, UT, and WI.

What distinguishes this location operationally is its strong customer satisfaction record: 481+ Google reviews averaging 5.0 out of 5 stars. Staff members are referenced repeatedly in reviews for being knowledgeable and professional. The secured lending model considers vehicle value alongside creditworthiness rather than relying solely on credit score, which broadens access for borrowers with damaged or limited credit histories. Borrowers retain use of their vehicle throughout the loan term.

Honest assessment: Title loans are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit, frequently carrying triple-digit annual percentage rates. TitleMax's website discloses no interest rates, fees, or APR ranges—borrowers must visit the store or call to understand actual costs. Using a vehicle title as collateral means that failure to repay can result in repossession of the borrower's vehicle, a serious consequence for lower-income borrowers who depend on their car for work. The flex line of credit and unsecured personal loan may offer more payment flexibility than a traditional single-balloon-payment title loan, but total cost comparisons should be made carefully before committing.
pros: Accepts most credit types — vehicle value is factored in, not credit score alone
Borrowers keep driving their vehicle while the loan is active
Bilingual staff available at this location
481+ Google reviews with a 5.0/5 average rating at the Memphis Summer Avenue branch
Three product types available at one location: title pledge, secured flex line of credit, and unsecured personal line of credit
Open six days a week including Saturdays
cons: No APR, fee, or rate information disclosed on the website — actual cost requires a store visit or phone call
Title loans carry inherent repossession risk — vehicle is collateral and can be seized for non-payment
Online unsecured loans restricted to 11 states; not available nationally
High-cost emergency lending category — title loans routinely carry triple-digit APRs
best_for: Borrowers with poor or thin credit who own their vehicle outright and need same-day cash
Consumers who cannot qualify for traditional unsecured personal loans
Memphis-area residents needing a revolving secured line of credit for recurring emergency expenses
Spanish-speaking borrowers in the Memphis area seeking bilingual in-person service
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for borrowers with poor credit who own their vehicle and urgently need cash — the secured lending model trades credit score requirements for vehicle collateral. The central caveat is cost and risk: rates are not disclosed online, title loans are among the most expensive credit products available, and non-payment can result in loss of the borrower's vehicle.
services: Flex line of credit (secured revolving credit backed by vehicle title)
Unsecured flexible line of credit (personal loan, in-store and online)
Credit review using vehicle value as approval factor
Incremental or lump-sum withdrawals from line of credit
Loan management via online customer portal
